title: PHP Operators
content:
### PHP Operators

Operators are used to perform operations on variables and values.

### Arithmetic Operators

| Operator | Name | Example | Result |
|----------|------|---------|--------|
| `+` | Addition | `5 + 3` | `8` |
| `-` | Subtraction | `5 - 3` | `2` |
| `*` | Multiplication | `5 * 3` | `15` |
| `/` | Division | `6 / 3` | `2` |
| `%` | Modulo | `5 % 2` | `1` |
| `**` | Exponentiation | `2 ** 3` | `8` |

### Comparison Operators

| Operator | Name |
|----------|------|
| `==` | Equal (value) |
| `===` | Identical (value and type) |
| `!=` | Not equal |
| `<>` | Not equal |
| `!==` | Not identical |
| `<`, `>`, `<=`, `>=` | Less/Greater than |

```php
<?php
$x = 10;
$y = 3;
echo $x + $y; // 13
echo $x % $y; // 1
echo $x ** $y; // 1000

// Loose vs strict comparison
var_dump(5 == "5");  // true (loose)
var_dump(5 === "5"); // false (strict)
?>
```

> **Key Point:** Always use `===` and `!==` for comparisons to avoid type coercion issues.
codeExample:
<?php
$a = 15;
$b = 4;
echo "Sum: " . ($a + $b) . "\n";
echo "Product: " . ($a * $b) . "\n";
echo "Power: " . ($a ** $b) . "\n";
var_dump($a == "15");  // true
var_dump($a === "15"); // false
?>

quiz.question: Which operator checks value AND type equality?
